panels.description: Wide shot: Shere Khan, massive and menacing, crouches low before the assembled Wolf Council in a moonlit clearing. His eyes glow fiercely. Akela and Bagheera look tense among the shadows of the surrounding wolves. Heavy shadows emphasize Shere Khan's dominance and threat.

bubbles.text: What have we to do with this toothless fool? He is doomed to die! It is the man-cub who has lived too long. Free People, he was my meat from the first. Give him to me. I am weary of this man-wolf folly. He has troubled the jungle for ten seasons. Give me the man-cub, or I will hunt here always, and not give you one bone. He is a man, a man's child, and from the marrow of my bones I hate him!

panels.description: Medium close-up on Akela's face. He looks stern but worried, holding his posture as the leader. Low, dramatic lighting under the jungle canopy.

bubbles.text: And turn all the people of the villages against us?
He has eaten our food. He has slept with us. He has driven game for us. He has broken no word of the Law of the Jungle.

panels.description: Close-up on Bagheera's wary eyes, looking toward Shere Khan. Expression is tense and watchful.

bubbles.text: He is our brother in all but blood,

panels.description: Extreme close-up on Shere Khan's open maw mid-howl, teeth bared aggressively. Motion lines convey intense, sudden sound and anger.

bubbles.text: He is a man--a man--a man!

panels.description: Wide shot focusing on Mowgli standing alone, centered among the wary wolves and angry Shere Khan. Mowgli is standing unnaturally upright, arms slightly away from his body, a mix of furious rage and deep sorrow crossing his face. The surrounding wolves look down or away.
Medium shot on Mowgli, seen slightly from below, asserting his new stance against the pack. His expression is defiant and bitter. The background is blurred shadows of the Council.

bubbles.text: Listen you! There is no need for this dog's jabber. Ye have told me so often tonight that I am a man (and indeed I would have been a wolf with you to my life's end) that I feel your words are true. So I do not call ye my brothers any more, but sag [dogs], as a man should.

panels.description: Action shot: Mowgli fiercely whirls a branch tipped with bright, crackling fire above his head. The flames cast dramatic, orange light on his determined face and frighten the surrounding wolves, who are recoiling in terror, backs arched away from the heat and light. Dynamic composition focusing on the fire's immediate impact.
